speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Sir, you have done enough, and have performed

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: A saintlike sorrow. No fault could you make

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Which you have not redeemed—indeed, paid down

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: More penitence than done trespass. At the last,

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Do as the heavens have done: forget your evil;

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: With them forgive yourself.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Whilst I remember

speaker: LEONTES
text: Her and her virtues, I cannot forget

speaker: LEONTES
text: My blemishes in them, and so still think of

speaker: LEONTES
text: The wrong I did myself, which was so much

speaker: LEONTES
text: That heirless it hath made my kingdom and

speaker: LEONTES
text: Destroyed the sweet’st companion that e’er man

speaker: LEONTES
text: Bred his hopes out of.

speaker: PAULINA
text: True, too true, my lord.

speaker: PAULINA
text: If one by one you wedded all the world,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Or from the all that are took something good

speaker: PAULINA
text: To make a perfect woman, she you killed

speaker: PAULINA
text: Would be unparalleled.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I think so. Killed?

speaker: LEONTES
text: She I killed? I did so, but thou strik’st me

speaker: LEONTES
text: Sorely to say I did. It is as bitter

speaker: LEONTES
text: Upon thy tongue as in my thought. Now, good now,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Say so but seldom.

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Not at all, good lady.

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: You might have spoken a thousand things that would

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Have done the time more benefit and graced

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Your kindness better.

speaker: PAULINA
text: You are one of those

speaker: PAULINA
text: Would have him wed again.

speaker: DION
text: If you would not so,

speaker: DION
text: You pity not the state nor the remembrance

speaker: DION
text: Of his most sovereign name, consider little

speaker: DION
text: What dangers by his Highness’ fail of issue

speaker: DION
text: May drop upon his kingdom and devour

speaker: DION
text: Incertain lookers-on. What were more holy

speaker: DION
text: Than to rejoice the former queen is well?

speaker: DION
text: What holier than, for royalty’s repair,

speaker: DION
text: For present comfort, and for future good,

speaker: DION
text: To bless the bed of majesty again

speaker: DION
text: With a sweet fellow to ’t?

speaker: PAULINA
text: There is none worthy,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Respecting her that’s gone. Besides, the gods

speaker: PAULINA
text: Will have fulfilled their secret purposes.

speaker: PAULINA
text: For has not the divine Apollo said,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Is ’t not the tenor of his oracle,

speaker: PAULINA
text: That King Leontes shall not have an heir

speaker: PAULINA
text: Till his lost child be found? Which that it shall

speaker: PAULINA
text: Is all as monstrous to our human reason

speaker: PAULINA
text: As my Antigonus to break his grave

speaker: PAULINA
text: And come again to me—who, on my life,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Did perish with the infant. ’Tis your counsel

speaker: PAULINA
text: My lord should to the heavens be contrary,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Oppose against their wills. Care not for issue.

speaker: PAULINA
text: The crown will find an heir. Great Alexander

speaker: PAULINA
text: Left his to th’ worthiest; so his successor

speaker: PAULINA
text: Was like to be the best.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Good Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Who hast the memory of Hermione,

speaker: LEONTES
text: I know, in honor, O, that ever I

speaker: LEONTES
text: Had squared me to thy counsel! Then even now

speaker: LEONTES
text: I might have looked upon my queen’s full eyes,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Have taken treasure from her lips—

speaker: PAULINA
text: And left them

speaker: PAULINA
text: More rich for what they yielded.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou speak’st truth.

speaker: LEONTES
text: No more such wives, therefore no wife. One worse,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And better used, would make her sainted spirit

speaker: LEONTES
text: Again possess her corpse, and on this stage,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where we offenders now appear, soul-vexed,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And begin “Why to me?”

speaker: PAULINA
text: Had she such power,

speaker: PAULINA
text: She had just cause.

speaker: LEONTES
text: She had, and would incense me

speaker: LEONTES
text: To murder her I married.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I should so.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Were I the ghost that walked, I’d bid you mark

speaker: PAULINA
text: Her eye, and tell me for what dull part in ’t

speaker: PAULINA
text: You chose her. Then I’d shriek, that even your ears

speaker: PAULINA
text: Should rift to hear me, and the words that followed

speaker: PAULINA
text: Should be “Remember mine.”

speaker: LEONTES
text: Stars, stars,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And all eyes else dead coals! Fear thou no wife;

speaker: LEONTES
text: I’ll have no wife, Paulina.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Will you swear

speaker: PAULINA
text: Never to marry but by my free leave?

speaker: LEONTES
text: Never, Paulina, so be blest my spirit.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Then, good my lords, bear witness to his oath.

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: You tempt him over-much.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Unless another

speaker: PAULINA
text: As like Hermione as is her picture

speaker: PAULINA
text: Affront his eye.

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Good madam—

speaker: PAULINA
text: I have done.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Yet if my lord will marry—if you will, sir,

speaker: PAULINA
text: No remedy but you will—give me the office

speaker: PAULINA
text: To choose you a queen. She shall not be so young

speaker: PAULINA
text: As was your former, but she shall be such

speaker: PAULINA
text: As, walked your first queen’s ghost, it should take joy

speaker: PAULINA
text: To see her in your arms.

speaker: LEONTES
text: My true Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: We shall not marry till thou bid’st us.

speaker: PAULINA
text: That

speaker: PAULINA
text: Shall be when your first queen’s again in breath,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Never till then.

speaker: SERVANT
text: One that gives out himself Prince Florizell,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Son of Polixenes, with his princess—she

speaker: SERVANT
text: The fairest I have yet beheld—desires access

speaker: SERVANT
text: To your high presence.

speaker: LEONTES
text: What with him? He comes not

speaker: LEONTES
text: Like to his father’s greatness. His approach,

speaker: LEONTES
text: So out of circumstance and sudden, tells us

speaker: LEONTES
text: ’Tis not a visitation framed, but forced

speaker: LEONTES
text: By need and accident. What train?

speaker: SERVANT
text: But few,

speaker: SERVANT
text: And those but mean.

speaker: LEONTES
text: His princess, say you, with him?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Ay, the most peerless piece of earth, I think,

speaker: SERVANT
text: That e’er the sun shone bright on.

speaker: PAULINA
text: O Hermione,

speaker: PAULINA
text: As every present time doth boast itself

speaker: PAULINA
text: Above a better gone, so must thy grave

speaker: PAULINA
text:
Give way to what’s seen now.
                 
                To
                 
                Servant
                .
               Sir, you yourself

speaker: PAULINA
text: Have said and writ so—but your writing now

speaker: PAULINA
text: Is colder than that theme—she had not been

speaker: PAULINA
text: Nor was not to be equalled. Thus your verse

speaker: PAULINA
text: Flowed with her beauty once. ’Tis shrewdly ebbed

speaker: PAULINA
text: To say you have seen a better.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Pardon, madam.

speaker: SERVANT
text: The one I have almost forgot—your pardon;

speaker: SERVANT
text: The other, when she has obtained your eye,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Will have your tongue too. This is a creature,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Would she begin a sect, might quench the zeal

speaker: SERVANT
text: Of all professors else, make proselytes

speaker: SERVANT
text: Of who she but bid follow.

speaker: PAULINA
text: How, not women?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Women will love her that she is a woman

speaker: SERVANT
text: More worth than any man; men, that she is

speaker: SERVANT
text: The rarest of all women.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Go, Cleomenes.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Yourself, assisted with your honored friends,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Bring them to our embracement.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Still, ’tis strange

speaker: LEONTES
text: He thus should steal upon us.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Had our prince,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Jewel of children, seen this hour, he had paired

speaker: PAULINA
text: Well with this lord. There was not full a month

speaker: PAULINA
text: Between their births.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Prithee, no more; cease. Thou know’st

speaker: LEONTES
text: He dies to me again when talked of. Sure,

speaker: LEONTES
text: When I shall see this gentleman, thy speeches

speaker: LEONTES
text: Will bring me to consider that which may

speaker: LEONTES
text: Unfurnish me of reason. They are come.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Your mother was most true to wedlock, prince,

speaker: LEONTES
text: For she did print your royal father off,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Conceiving you. Were I but twenty-one,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Your father’s image is so hit in you,

speaker: LEONTES
text: His very air, that I should call you brother,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As I did him, and speak of something wildly

speaker: LEONTES
text: By us performed before. Most dearly welcome,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And your fair princess—goddess! O, alas,

speaker: LEONTES
text: I lost a couple that ’twixt heaven and Earth

speaker: LEONTES
text: Might thus have stood, begetting wonder, as

speaker: LEONTES
text: You, gracious couple, do. And then I lost—

speaker: LEONTES
text: All mine own folly—the society,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Amity too, of your brave father, whom,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Though bearing misery, I desire my life

speaker: LEONTES
text: Once more to look on him.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: By his command

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Have I here touched Sicilia, and from him

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Give you all greetings that a king, at friend,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Can send his brother. And but infirmity,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Which waits upon worn times, hath something seized

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: His wished ability, he had himself

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: The lands and waters ’twixt your throne and his

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Measured to look upon you, whom he loves—

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: He bade me say so—more than all the scepters

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: And those that bear them living.

speaker: LEONTES
text: O my brother,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Good gentleman, the wrongs I have done thee stir

speaker: LEONTES
text: Afresh within me, and these thy offices,

speaker: LEONTES
text: So rarely kind, are as interpreters

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of my behindhand slackness. Welcome hither,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As is the spring to th’ earth. And hath he too

speaker: LEONTES
text: Exposed this paragon to th’ fearful usage,

speaker: LEONTES
text: At least ungentle, of the dreadful Neptune,

speaker: LEONTES
text: To greet a man not worth her pains, much less

speaker: LEONTES
text: Th’ adventure of her person?

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Good my lord,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: She came from Libya.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where the warlike Smalus,

speaker: LEONTES
text: That noble honored lord, is feared and loved?

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Most royal sir, from thence, from him, whose daughter

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: His tears proclaimed his, parting with her. Thence,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: A prosperous south wind friendly, we have crossed

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: To execute the charge my father gave me

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: For visiting your Highness. My best train

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: I have from your Sicilian shores dismissed,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Who for Bohemia bend, to signify

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Not only my success in Libya, sir,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: But my arrival and my wife’s in safety

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Here where we are.

speaker: LEONTES
text: The blessèd gods

speaker: LEONTES
text: Purge all infection from our air whilst you

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do climate here! You have a holy father,

speaker: LEONTES
text: A graceful gentleman, against whose person,

speaker: LEONTES
text: So sacred as it is, I have done sin,

speaker: LEONTES
text: For which the heavens, taking angry note,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Have left me issueless. And your father’s blest,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As he from heaven merits it, with you,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Worthy his goodness. What might I have been

speaker: LEONTES
text: Might I a son and daughter now have looked on,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Such goodly things as you?

speaker: LORD
text: Most noble sir,

speaker: LORD
text: That which I shall report will bear no credit,

speaker: LORD
text: Were not the proof so nigh. Please you, great sir,

speaker: LORD
text: Bohemia greets you from himself by me,

speaker: LORD
text: Desires you to attach his son, who has—

speaker: LORD
text: His dignity and duty both cast off—

speaker: LORD
text: Fled from his father, from his hopes, and with

speaker: LORD
text: A shepherd’s daughter.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where’s Bohemia? Speak.

speaker: LORD
text: Here in your city. I now came from him.

speaker: LORD
text: I speak amazedly, and it becomes

speaker: LORD
text: My marvel and my message. To your court

speaker: LORD
text: Whiles he was hast’ning—in the chase, it seems,

speaker: LORD
text: Of this fair couple—meets he on the way

speaker: LORD
text: The father of this seeming lady and

speaker: LORD
text: Her brother, having both their country quitted

speaker: LORD
text: With this young prince.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Camillo has betrayed me,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Whose honor and whose honesty till now

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Endured all weathers.

speaker: LORD
text: Lay ’t so to his charge.

speaker: LORD
text: He’s with the King your father.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Who? Camillo?

speaker: LORD
text: Camillo, sir. I spake with him, who now

speaker: LORD
text: Has these poor men in question. Never saw I

speaker: LORD
text: Wretches so quake. They kneel, they kiss the earth,

speaker: LORD
text: Forswear themselves as often as they speak.

speaker: LORD
text: Bohemia stops his ears and threatens them

speaker: LORD
text: With divers deaths in death.

speaker: PERDITA
text: O my poor father!

speaker: PERDITA
text: The heaven sets spies upon us, will not have

speaker: PERDITA
text: Our contract celebrated.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You are married?

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: We are not, sir, nor are we like to be.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: The stars, I see, will kiss the valleys first.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: The odds for high and low’s alike.

speaker: LEONTES
text: My lord,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is this the daughter of a king?

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: She is

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: When once she is my wife.

speaker: LEONTES
text: That “once,” I see, by your good father’s speed

speaker: LEONTES
text: Will come on very slowly. I am sorry,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Most sorry, you have broken from his liking,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where you were tied in duty, and as sorry

speaker: LEONTES
text: Your choice is not so rich in worth as beauty,

speaker: LEONTES
text: That you might well enjoy her.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Dear, look up.

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Though Fortune, visible an enemy,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Should chase us with my father, power no jot

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Hath she to change our loves.—Beseech you, sir,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Remember since you owed no more to time

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Than I do now. With thought of such affections,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: Step forth mine advocate. At your request,

speaker: FLORIZELL
text: My father will grant precious things as trifles.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Would he do so, I’d beg your precious mistress,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which he counts but a trifle.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Sir, my liege,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Your eye hath too much youth in ’t. Not a month

speaker: PAULINA
text: ’Fore your queen died, she was more worth such gazes

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than what you look on now.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I thought of her

speaker: LEONTES
text:
Even in these looks I made.
                 
                To
                 
                Florizell
                .
               But your petition

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is yet unanswered. I will to your father.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Your honor not o’erthrown by your desires,

speaker: LEONTES
text: I am friend to them and you. Upon which errand

speaker: LEONTES
text: I now go toward him. Therefore follow me,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And mark what way I make. Come, good my lord.
